sourceScope.primarySourceFile: AG 13 - Guideline Concerning the Commissioners' Annuity Reserve Valuation Method.pdf
sourceScope.sourceTitle: AG 13 - Guideline Concerning the Commissioners' Annuity Reserve Valuation Method

observedSectionWindows.note: Two-page historical CARVM guidance on contingent surrender charges and bail-out rate treatment.

boundaries: Keep planning focused on this single AG 13 PDF.
Do not widen into AG 14 or later guideline files.
Keep the historical CARVM posture explicit in every batch artifact.
Treat the guideline as caveat-first historical guidance, not learner-facing policy text.
Keep any PDF text-extraction noise visible as a caveat, not a promotion signal.
Keep the batch source-bound and review-only.
exclusions: No eLearning scripts, slides, quizzes, or app exports.
No raw source files in Git.
No other guideline files in the same wave.

topicMap.label: Historical CARVM guidance on contingent surrender charges and bail-out rates
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep this slice within pages 1-2 and do not absorb later guideline files.

proposedBatches.title: Two-page AG 13 historical CARVM guidance

pageTarget.notes: Capture the full two-page historical guideline as a single review-only slice and stop at the page boundary.

proposedBatches.rationale: Capture the full two-page historical guideline as a single review-only slice and stop at the page boundary.

reviewStandards.regulatoryRequirement: Treat explicit must, shall, or required language as the requirement; preserve the citation and source context.
reviewStandards.definitionOrTerminology: Treat CARVM, contingent surrender charge, bail-out rate, and guarantee language as historical context unless they impose a current duty.
reviewStandards.caveatOnlyHistoricalGuidance: Treat the historical framing around contingent surrender charges as caveat-first guidance, not active policy text.
reviewStandards.crossReferenceMapping: Treat references to CARVM, guaranteed duration, external index values, cash surrender values, or severe hardship as mapping notes unless operational text is included.
reviewStandards.backgroundContent: Treat the title line and framing sentence as background content unless they impose a duty.
reviewStandards.boundaryControlWindow: Treat the two-page slice as a review-only window that should not be merged forward.
reviewStandards.requiresHumanInterpretation: Treat the indexing choice and the OCR / encoded text layer as requiring human actuarial interpretation unless the text is explicit.
reviewStandards.sourceQualityCaveat: Treat text-layer noise as review-only caveats and do not widen the batch because of them.

appReady: export is sanitized, stable, and versioned
ragReady: review status no longer requires human disposition

currentChecksNeeded: Plan file exists and parses as JSON.
The AG 13 plan markdown exists and carries the expected headings.
Every proposed batch is review-only by default.
The plan keeps the scope to two pages only.
The source posture is explicitly historical.
Each planned batch has a matching batch definition.
The AG 13 batch definition file stays synchronized with the plan.
futureChecksSuggested: Two-page guideline checks for future short guideline files.
No learner-facing, app-ready, or RAG-ready promotion checks.
Text-layer quality caveat checks once extraction starts.
scriptImplications: scripts/ag13-batch-definitions.mjs should stay synchronized with the planned batch-091 slice when extraction is authorized.
scripts/batch-definitions.mjs should import the AG 13 definition file so the shared runner can find the planned batch ID.
scripts/validate-scaffold.mjs should verify that the AG 13 plan file exists, parses cleanly, and preserves the review-only posture.
The shared runner should continue to honor the same stop conditions used for the VM-20 batches.

notes: Controlled AG 13 planning artifact. Planned batch 091 remains review-only by default and is not an extraction run.
